rulings.Case Title: JOSEPH D. STEWART VS. AIRBNB INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION TO VACATE ARBITRATION
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Wednesday, July 31, 2024, Line 1, PLAINTIFF JOSEPH STEWART'S PETITION TO VACATE ARBITRATION. Ordered off calendar. Plaintiff concedes he late-filed his memorandum. (Rply. 2:4-15.) If plaintiff elects to re-set the matter for hearing, he is ordered to comply with Local Rule 2.7(B) (courtesy copies). Plaintiff's papers shall bear the new hearing date, which shall be a Mon.-Thur. after August 15. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: STEVEN FREDIANI VS. PACIFIC GAS AND ELECTRIC COMPANY, A CALIFORNIA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Wednesday, July 31, 2024, Line 2, PLAINTIFFS STEVEN FREDIANI, ROBERT RIGLEY, RICHARD TROCHE, AND MICHAEL DION'S MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE. Hearing required re apparent absence of Bailey pro hac vice declaration. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: AMERICAN ACCOUNTABILITY FOUNDATION VS. THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Petition For Writ Of Mandate And Declaratory Relief Under The California Public Records Act
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Wednesday, July 31, 2024, Line 9, PLAINTIFF AMERICAN ACCOUNTABILITY FOUNDATION'w Petition For Writ Of Mandate And Declaratory Relief Under The California Public Records Act. Hearing required re absence of opposition. Also, parties are reminded of Local Rule 2.7(B) (courtesy copies). For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL J. NILAND VS. NUPORT CONSTRUCTION, INC., A CALIFORNIA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Wednesday, July 31, 2024, Line 10, 2- DEFENDANT NUPORT CONSTRUCTION, INC.'S DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT. Off calendar. The Hern declaration fails to show that the parties met and conferred "in person, by telephone, or by video conference" in compliance with CCP 430.41. The parties are ordered to comply with the code. The response to the complaint is now due August 28, 2024. =(302/RBU)
